name: Abandoned village of Granadilla - Aldeanueva del Camino y Lagunilla — circular through the Dehesa del Coto
name_original: Pueblo abandonado de Granadilla - Aldeanueva del Camino y Lagunilla — circular por la Dehesa del Coto
region_intro: Road cycling around Montemayor Del Río is characterized by its location between the Sierra de Béjar and Sierra de Francia, offering diverse mountainous terrain. The region features challenging ascents with gradients exceeding 15%, alongside scenic paths through chestnut forests and along the Cuerpo de Hombre river. Cyclists encounter varied landscapes, from dense woodlands to areas with impressive granite rock formations. This blend provides a rewarding experience for road cyclists seeking elevation and natural beauty. Best road cycling routes around Montemayor Del Río The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de Béjar y Béjar desde Harvás – circular por Montemayor del Río, a 49.3 miles (79.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Candelario Climbs Loop, a difficult 67.7 miles (108.9 km) path. It offers sustained climbs and panoramic views across the Sierra de Candelario. Local road cyclists also love the Abandoned village of Granadilla - Aldeanueva del Camino y Lagunilla — circular through the Dehesa del Coto, a 45.3 miles (72.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including historical sites and oak forests, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Montemayor Del Río is defined by challenging mountain passes, dense chestnut forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult, long-distance climbs. The routes in Montemayor Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 19 reviews. More than 2700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Montemayor Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Montemayor del Río?
A: Road cycling around Montemayor del Río offers diverse mountainous terrain, situated between the Sierra de Béjar and Sierra de Francia. You'll encounter challenging ascents, some with gradients exceeding 15%, alongside scenic paths through dense chestnut forests and along the Cuerpo de Hombre river. The landscape varies from woodlands to areas with impressive granite rock formations, providing a rewarding experience for cyclists seeking elevation and natural beauty.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the Montemayor del Río area?
A: Yes, Montemayor del Río is known as an epicenter for challenging road cycling. The area features several ascents of considerable difficulty, including very tough ramps that can exceed a 15% gradient. Notable climbs like the Alto del Cordel and Alto de Aldeacipreste provide significant challenges for experienced riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023280187/sierra-de-candelario-climbs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Candelario Climbs Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 108.9 km path with over 2500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are some scenic natural features or viewpoints I can discover while cycling?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through spectacular chestnut forests, especially vibrant in autumn, and along the Cuerpo de Hombre river, shaded by ancient oak trees. The area also features impressive granite rock formations. For stunning vistas, consider routes that pass viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/656367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montemayor and the Baños Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Montemayor del Río is a Historic-Artistic Site with several notable attractions. You can explore the imposing 13th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1572135/attractions-around-montemayor-del-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Vicente&lt;/a&gt;, also known as 'Castillo del Paraíso', or the 13th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1572135/attractions-around-montemayor-del-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de Nuestra Señora de la Asunción&lt;/a&gt;. Routes may also pass by Roman milestones, such as those highlighted on the Ruta de los Miliarios, or historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2812934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;"La Cruz" on the ancient Roman road Calzada Baños de Montemayor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Montemayor del Río?
A: The diverse landscapes of Montemayor del Río are enjoyable across seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling. Spring brings lush greenery, while autumn transforms the chestnut forests into spectacular displays of color. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winter rides are possible but require checking weather conditions, especially for higher elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, there are options suitable for varying abilities. For a more relaxed experience, the nearby Vía Verde (Greenway) from Baños de Montemayor offers dedicated, flatter paths ideal for families. Within the komoot selection, there are 3 easy and 39 moderate routes, which may include sections suitable for families or those seeking less strenuous rides.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a road cycling route?
A: Montemayor del Río, as a village, typically offers public parking areas. It's advisable to look for designated parking within the village center or near the start points of specific routes. For routes that begin outside the village, such as those near Baños de Montemayor, parking facilities are usually available in those towns or at trailheads.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Montemayor del Río?
A: The road cycling routes in Montemayor del Río are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 19 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the beauty of the dense chestnut forests, and the varied river valleys. Over 2700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the region's diverse terrain, indicating a strong appreciation for the area's offerings.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Montemayor del Río are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056223805/puerto-de-bejar-y-bejar-desde-harvas-circular-por-montemayor-del-rio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Béjar y Béjar desde Harvás – circular por Montemayor del Río&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025197369/abandoned-village-of-granadilla-aldeanueva-del-camino-y-lagunilla-circular-through-the-dehesa-del-coto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned village of Granadilla - Aldeanueva del Camino y Lagunilla — circular through the Dehesa del Coto&lt;/a&gt;. These routes offer diverse landscapes and return you to your starting point.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the cycling routes or in Montemayor del Río?
A: Montemayor del Río and nearby towns offer amenities for cyclists. You'll find cafes and pubs in the village center, such as those around the Plaza Mayor. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/987118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pequeña Estación Bar Restaurant Café&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that cyclists might encounter. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages, and plan your stops accordingly.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Montemayor del Río area?
A: There are over 90 road cycling routes available around Montemayor del Río on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 3 easy, 39 moderate, and 54 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for almost every road cyclist.

Q: Can I access Montemayor del Río by public transport with my bike?
A: While Montemayor del Río is a rural area, public transport options exist to nearby larger towns. It's essential to check with specific bus or train operators regarding their policies for transporting bicycles, as this can vary. Often, regional buses may allow bikes if space permits, but it's best to confirm in advance.
tips: Here is a nice little town. The population does not reach 900 inhabitants but it has a hostel for cyclists. It is a good place to start an excursion or to stop during an excursion.
It is an abandoned town, in the process of recovery, with a lot of history and heritage.

Belonging to the Sierra de Béjar, very close to the province of Cáceres, the settlement in the municipality of Lagunilla refers us to the time of the castros, whose remains, including burial mounds, were found in the Valley of Hornacinos
